This only my experience, I cannot speak for anyone else.  And I only share because this is included in the Netflix show.  At first, there was shock/surprise and my arms 'startled' up, then I tried to grab hold of something to stop the momentum.  But I was sleeping in a pile of sheets on the floor of the room next to mine, and neither they nor the pillow provided me an anchor on which to fasten myself.  I began to scream for help, one of the people in the room turned on the lights, and the motion stopped.  Being in a haunting is difficult, primarily, because you begin to question the lens by which you see the world.  In actuality, I was laying face down, but it looks like the producers felt that a face up scene was more powerful?  Slight variation from the moment itself, but they did effectively capture the fell. I hope that you enjoy the show, and please be well.

Being the one that endured that moment - I can share with you that it did leave me with PTSD.  After that day, March 10th, 1985, I would instead walk across the entire campus in order to use the group showers in the sports locker room. I felt safer that way, and felt that having possible witnesses present would deter the thing's aggressivness.  Sadly, I think that many people quietly suffer from life's challenges whether it be personal loss, poverty, addiction, etc. and finding ways to function beyond that pain is an important goal.  Stay safe.  Spoiler alert: I live. :)
